commentaries.author: Douay Rheims 1609
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 1 Chronicles 22:3 And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails for the doors of the gates, and for the fittings; and bronze in abundance without weight; Douay Rheims 1609 The son of Sechenias: Semeia, whose sons were Hattus, and Jegaal, and Baria, and Naaria, and Saphat, (b) six in number. (b) Semeia with his five sons are counted six sons of Sechenias, though Semeia only was his proper son, the other his nephews.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh 1 Chronicles 22:3 And David prepared iron in abundance for the nails for the doors of the gates, and for the fittings; and bronze in abundance without weight; Prepared. Syriac and Arabic, "appointed blacksmiths from among the proselytes, to forge tools for cutting and dressing stone "But most follow the Vulgate. (Calmet) Closures is explained by the following word, which alone occurs in Hebrew Immense. Hebrew and Septuagint, "abundance, it was not weighed.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
